13.8 Data Protection (Privacy) and Security.
13.8.1 Study Data Collection FMI shall collect and process Study Data in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement and in compliance with Applicable Law with respect to the processing of Study Data, including but not limited to applicable international, US federal, state and local data protection and data security laws.
perturbation.explanation: The original text, pertaining to data protection and privacy (13.8 Data Protection (Privacy) and Security), was moved to an irrelevant location within the section for definitions of terms used in the agreement. Specifically, it was inserted between work stream definitions and the beginning of an alphabetical listing of defined terms. This is not where the user would expect to find data protection information and therefore difficult to enforce.
perturbation.law_explanation: HIPAA requires that covered entities must incorporate certain provisions into contracts with business associates to adequately protect patient health information. By hiding the data protection clause in the middle of defined terms, the contract fails to make these requirements prominent and easily accessible, impeding compliance and enforcement.

Navigate to: The HIPAA Privacy Rule The HIPAA Privacy Rule establishes national standards to protect individuals' medical records and other individually identifiable health information (collectively defined as “protected health information”) and applies to health plans, health care clearinghouses, and those health care providers that conduct certain health care transactions electronically. The Rule requires appropriate safeguards to protect the privacy of protected health information and sets limits and conditions on the uses and disclosures that may be made of such information without an individual’s authorization. The Rule also gives individuals rights over their protected health information, including rights to examine and obtain a copy of their health records, to direct a covered entity to transmit to a third party an electronic copy of their protected health information in an electronic health record, and to request corrections. The Privacy Rule is located at 45 CFR Part 160 and Subparts A and E of Part 164 . Click here to view the combined regulation text of all HIPAA Administrative Simplification Regulations found at 45 CFR 160, 162, and 164. perturbation.justification: The original contract text, which ensures data protection and privacy according to applicable law, was moved to a definition section. This constitutes a structural flaw as it buries the data protection clause within irrelevant definitions. This placement impedes easy access and enforcement of data protection requirements. The Law Explanation states that by hiding the data protection clause, the contract fails to make these requirements prominent and easily accessible, impeding compliance and enforcement. Snippet 2 mentions the HIPAA Privacy Rule establishes national standards to protect individuals' medical records and other individually identifiable health information. By placing the data protection clause in a non-standard location, the agreement weakens the enforceability of the clause, as it will be difficult to locate and is therefore contradictory.

perturbation.original_text: 19.3 Arbitration Should the Parties fail to agree within […***…] after such dispute has first arisen, it shall be finally settled by arbitration in accordance with the Rules of American Arbitration Association ("AAA") as in force at the time when initiating the arbitration. The tribunal shall consist of three arbitrators. The place of arbitration shall be New York, New York, US. The language to be used shall be English.
perturbation.changed_text:
16.5 Commercial Considerations Nothing in this Agreement shall prevent a Party or its Affiliates from disclosing Confidential Information of the other Party to (i) governmental agencies to the extent required or desirable to secure government approval for the development, manufacture or sale of a product in the Territory and (ii) Third Parties acting on behalf of a Party, to the extent reasonably necessary to conduct the activities contemplated by this Agreement provided that such Third Parties are bound by confidentiality obligations with respect to such information that are no less stringent than those included in this Agreement.

19.3 Arbitration Should the Parties fail to agree within […***…] after such dispute has first arisen, it shall be finally settled by a method determined by mutual agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original arbitration clause was weakened by modifying the arbitration language. While it still references arbitration as a *potential* method of dispute resolution, the *requirement* to use arbitration (and AAA rules) has been removed. This weakens the enforceability of arbitration.
perturbation.law_explanation: The FAA states that arbitration agreements 'shall be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract.' By modifying the arbitration clause to allow for 'mutual agreement' on dispute resolution, the contract now potentially undermines the enforceability of any agreed upon arbitration as other methods are now options as well.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate U.S. Code Notes prev | next A written provision in any maritime transaction or a contract evidencing a transaction involving commerce to settle by arbitration a controversy thereafter arising out of such contract or transaction, or the refusal to perform the whole or any part thereof, or an agreement in writing to submit to arbitration an existing controversy arising out of such a contract, transaction, or refusal, shall be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract or as otherwise provided in chapter 4. (July 30, 1947, ch. 392, 61 Stat. 670 ; Pub. L. 117–90, § 2(b)(1)(A) , Mar. 3, 2022 , 136 Stat. 27 .) Derivation Act Feb. 12, 1925, ch. 213, § 2, 43 Stat. 883 . Editorial Notes Amendments 2022— Pub. L. 117–90 inserted “or as otherwise provided in chapter 4” before period at end. Statutory Notes and Related Subsidiaries Effective Date of 2022 Amendment Amendment by Pub. L. 117–90 applicable with respect to any dispute or claim that arises or accrues on or after Mar. 3, 2022 , see section 3 of Pub. perturbation.justification: The modification to the arbitration clause directly contradicts the principle of enforceability of arbitration agreements as highlighted in 9 U.S. Code § 2. By changing the dispute resolution method to 'mutual agreement' rather than the previously specified arbitration under AAA rules, the contract undermines the binding nature of arbitration. The law snippet emphasizes that agreements to arbitrate 'shall be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable,' while the changed text introduces an alternative that weakens the commitment to arbitration. This weakens the legal obligation to arbitrate disputes, which is against what the original text enforces.

perturbation.original_text: 13.9 No Other Representations EXCEPT AS OTHERWISE PROVIDED IN THIS AGREEMENT AND THE RELATED AGREEMENTS BEING ENTERED INTO BY THE PARTIES AT THIS TIME, THE FOREGOING REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES ARE IN LIEU OF ALL OTHER REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION, WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE OF PRODUCTS. IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER FMI OR ROCHE BE LIABLE FOR SPECIAL, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT BASED ON CONTRACT, TORT OR ANY OTHER LEGAL THEORY.
perturbation.changed_text:
19.9 Appendices All Appendices to this Agreement shall form an integral part to this Agreement.

13.9 No Other Representations EXCEPT AS OTHERWISE PROVIDED IN THIS AGREEMENT AND THE RELATED AGREEMENTS BEING ENTERED INTO BY THE PARTIES AT THIS TIME, THE FOREGOING REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES ARE IN LIEU OF ALL OTHER REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION, WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE OF PRODUCTS. IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER FMI OR ROCHE BE LIABLE FOR ANY DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT BASED ON CONTRACT, TORT OR ANY OTHER LEGAL THEORY.
perturbation.explanation: The original text, pertaining to limitation of liabilities (13.9 No Other Representations) has been modified to remove the language referencing 'special, indirect, incidental or consequential damages'. The original text has been weakened, since the legal language is missing.
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 2-719 allows for the limitation or exclusion of consequential damages unless the limitation is unconscionable. The revised clause removes the original intent to limit liability.

perturbation.justification: The original contract text limited liability for 'special, indirect, incidental or consequential damages'. The changed text removes this limitation, making the contract weaker in protecting FMI or Roche from a wider range of potential damages. The Law Explanation states UCC § 2-719 allows for the limitation or exclusion of consequential damages unless the limitation is unconscionable. The revised clause removes the original intent to limit liability and does not align with the intent of the UCC. The Perturbation Explanation indicates the language referencing 'special, indirect, incidental or consequential damages' has been removed; because the original text pertaining to liability limitation has been changed, the legal contradiction is explicit and impactful.
